title: Or else
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: At 12 cups it says Silver — correct. Change it to 30 and see what happens.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
why.changed-data: You changed the number of cups.
hint2.changed-data: The data stays at 12. You can change it while testing, but put it back at the end.
hint2.wrong-shape: The three tiers stay. What changes is how they are joined, not how many there are.
hint2.no-else: Three separate checks all run. They should be one decision with three outcomes.
hint3.no-else: else means otherwise. It attaches to the previous if and only runs if that one did not.
hint4.no-else: Join the three with else if, and add one else at the end for someone with fewer than five cups.
hint2.wrong-tier: Twelve cups is between ten and twenty-five.
hint3.wrong-tier: Joined conditions are checked top to bottom and stop at the first true one. So their order matters — the highest tier has to be at the top.
hint2.wrong-discount: It goes with the tier. Fix the tier and it will fix itself.
why.wrong-count: There are not two lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Three loyalty tiers. Five cups gives Bronze, ten gives Silver, twenty-five gives Gold.
blocks.1.text: At twelve cups it says Silver. Correct. Change it to 30 and it says Gold — also correct. It looks like the code works.
blocks.3.text: The three conditions are not joined. Each is checked separately, and at 30 cups all three pass — Bronze, then Silver, then Gold, one on top of the other.
blocks.4.text: The right answer comes out only because Gold is last and overwrites the others. Swap the order and everything falls apart: a customer with thirty cups gets Bronze, because that was written at the bottom.
blocks.6.text: It joins conditions into one decision. They are checked top to bottom, it stops at the first true one, and the rest are never even looked at.
blocks.8.text: Here one of the two definitely runs. Not both, and not neither.
blocks.9.text: Order is now part of the logic
blocks.10.text: With joined conditions, order is not a matter of tidiness. It is part of the meaning.
blocks.11.text: The condition for five cups is also true at thirty. If it comes first, it stops there and nobody ever reaches Gold.
blocks.12.quote: The narrowest condition goes at the top.
blocks.14.text: Join the three conditions into one decision and add the case for someone with fewer than five cups: tier None, discount zero.
blocks.15.text: Test with 30, with 12, with 7 and with 2. All four should be right. Then put the cups back to 12.
